description: Byte-exact regression pins for hooks/_lib/shim-cache.sh shim_cache_key. 0.49.0 Phase 1b introduced cache-key schema v2 for the opt-in global-CLI resolver tier (3 new fields: trust_tier, registry_mtime, registry_size; enforce_cli_shape carries the EFFECTIVE value). The key is the first 32 hex chars of sha256(NUL-join(args)), so these values are deterministic across macOS and Linux. A change here means the cache-key construction changed (NUL-join order / field set) and MUST be a deliberate regen — every v2 entry on disk silently invalidates, which is fine (tmpfs + 3600s TTL) but the pin forces the change to be intentional. The v1 rows are negatives: a v1-shaped 14-arg call no longer produces a key (the guard rose from 14 to 17), and a 17-arg call with schema_version v1 produces a key that can never collide with a v2 lookup (schema is field 1, re-checked by the read-side validator).

rows.label: v2 project tier — registry fields empty
v2 global tier — registry mtime/size present, effective enforce_cli_shape=1
v1 negative (17-arg, schema_version v1) — distinct key, never matches a v2 lookup
v1 negative (legacy 14-arg call shape) — the v2 guard rejects fewer than 17 args
